findings.problem: The manuscript's sheet count is internally repeated as seventeen, but the chapter boundary handoff for Chapter 4 establishes fifty-seven sheets. This is a hard continuity contradiction on a central object and will make the next chapter read as if the manuscript changed size off-page.
findings.rewrite_direction: Align the manuscript count everywhere it appears in the discovery/removal beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:59, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:61, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:65, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:75 so Chapter 3 matches the canonical count used at the Chapter 4 boundary.
findings.acceptance_test: A reader comparing the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:59, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:61, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:65, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:75 with the Chapter 4 handoff finds one consistent manuscript total and no conflicting count anywhere in Chapter 3.

findings.problem: The decoding half of the chapter documents obsession, but too many consecutive beats cash out to the same result: the marks are structured, meaningful, and unreadable. Because the methods change more than the dramatic consequence changes, the chapter's second movement plateaus into a work log instead of escalating scene pressure to shortlist-level force.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the failed-decoding sequence beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:83, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:89, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:91, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:145, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:147, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:165 so each retained attempt produces a distinct turn: one new inference, one sharper false breakthrough, one clearer social or academic cost, and one relapse beat that lands harder than the prior loop. Cut or fuse any attempt that only restates unreadability.
findings.acceptance_test: Reading the revised run from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:83 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:183, each major decoding beat changes the chapter in a visible way: either Gavin learns a genuinely new thing, misreads a concrete clue that worsens the obsession, or pays a newly rendered cost in school or friendship. If two adjacent beats only restate that the marks are organized but opaque, the passage fails.

findings.problem: The uncanny description leans too often on the same subtractive formula of chained negation: telling the reader what the light, stone, material, or marks are not instead of what Gavin can positively perceive. Once the pattern appears this many times in one chapter, it stops feeling like controlled emphasis and starts reading as an autopilot prose tic.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the descriptive beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:19, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:21, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:29, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:39, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:45, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:55, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:99 so most of them give affirmative sensory information rooted in Gavin's frame of reference rather than multi-item 'not X, not Y' stacks. Keep only the strongest one or two negation beats.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:19, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:21, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:29, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:39, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:45, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:55, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:99, no more than two uncanny-description beats rely on chained negation; the rest identify texture, motion, temperature, geometry, or effect directly enough that a reader can picture what Gavin is perceiving without being told a list of rejected categories.

findings.problem: The narration uses clipped fragments such as dropped-subject or noun-phrase sentences to manufacture terseness. That violates the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration and makes the rhythm feel authored rather than organic to Gavin's close-third voice.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the clipped beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:17, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:35, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:57 into fully grammatical sentences with explicit subjects and finite verbs, preserving the pace through sentence length and sequencing rather than fragmentary shorthand.
findings.acceptance_test: At the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:17, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:35, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:57, each sentence reads as a complete clause with an explicit subject and verb. If any of those beats still depend on subjectless narration fragments or stand-alone noun phrases for emphasis, the passage fails.

summary: Dialogue stays character-true to the style bible, with Gavin evasive, Miren direct, and Duncan spare. The chapter still fails review because a high-stakes manuscript-count contradiction breaks continuity, the decoding section plateaus instead of escalating, and the prose leans too hard on repeated negation stacks and constitution-banned clipped narration.
